preamble: The shift from the open-mid front unrounded vowel [ɛ] to the back-closing diphthong [au] involves both backing and diphthongization. This can occur in complex vowel shifts where front vowels are pulled towards backer positions.

examples.note: Phonetic backing and diphthongization in specific stress environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
